mnemonics: Dotted Decimals are Like Phone Numbers: Area Code.Network.Host.Subnet (A.N.H.S.)
CIDR Slash Shows the Mask: /24 means 255.255.255.0, the network's task.
Private IPs are Like Internal Extensions: 10.x, 172.16-31, 192.168.x - for your LAN connections.
keyPoints: An IPv4 address is a 32-bit numerical label assigned to a device on a network, represented in dotted-decimal notation (e.g., 192.168.1.1).
The address is divided into a Network portion (identifying the network) and a Host portion (identifying the specific device on that network), defined by a subnet mask.
Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) notation (e.g., /24) simplifies the representation of the subnet mask and allows for more flexible network sizes than the older classful system (A, B, C, D, E).
commonMistakes: Confusing the network address (first address in a range, host bits all 0s) and the broadcast address (last address, host bits all 1s) with usable host addresses.
Incorrectly calculating the number of hosts in a subnet by forgetting to subtract 2 (for the network and broadcast addresses) from the total available addresses (2^n - 2).
Mistaking a public IP address (globally routable on the internet) for a private IP address (reserved for internal networks and requiring NAT to communicate externally).
